skill.name: truth-layer
skill.description: Adds an honesty-first verification layer for checking claims, builds, tests, type safety, and blocker reports before work is marked complete.
skill.summary: Verify project claims and surface blockers before reporting completion.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static Ruby backtick and weak cryptography detections are false positives from Markdown fences, inline command text, and the word "blocker" in SKILL.md. The skill does not contain executable code, but it instructs agents to run Bash commands and use filesystem, git, and Playwright MCP tools, so marketplace users should understand the operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Broad Tool Use Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to use Playwright MCP, filesystem MCP, git MCP, and Bash when blocked. This is a legitimate verification workflow, but it can inspect local state and run build commands if the host grants those tools.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly call for filesystem, git, UI inspection, and Bash usage. I found no malicious intent or exfiltration, but the requested capabilities are broad enough to require a marketplace warning.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Detections Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections occur in Markdown code fences and inline documentation examples, not in executable Ruby code. They do not create a command execution path by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is Markdown documentation and report templates. There is no Ruby file, shell script, or executable wrapper in the analyzed file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detection Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography hit points to the frontmatter description text, which says "When you discover a blocker:". No cryptographic algorithm or crypto API appears at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain metadata text and does not reference MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any crypto implementation. The finding does not match the file content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Command Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells the agent to run actual build commands and gives npm run typecheck as an example. This is useful for verification, but command execution should remain scoped to the active project.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command guidance is explicit and semantically relevant. It appears operational rather than malicious, so the risk is moderate.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when status reports claim success before builds, tests, and types are actually checked. This skill adds a verification checkpoint that reports blockers clearly and prevents premature completion claims.
actual_capabilities: Checks claims against actual project state before work is marked complete.
Requires build, test, dependency, and type safety verification when relevant.
Identifies blocker conditions that should stop dependent work.
Formats blocker reports with failure, impact, root cause, current state, and next step.
Prompts agents to inspect UI state, file integrity, and git history when blocked.
limitations: It depends on available tools and project commands in the active environment.
It does not provide automated scanners or executable validation scripts.
It can slow workflows when strict verification is not required.
It cannot prove production readiness without real tests and deployment checks.

use_cases.title: Validate Engineering Status
use_cases.description: Confirm that build, test, and type claims are backed by real evidence before reporting completion.

use_cases.title: Review Agent Output
use_cases.description: Catch optimistic or incomplete agent reports before they become project status updates.

use_cases.title: Document Release Blockers
use_cases.description: Create clear blocker reports that explain what failed, why it matters, and what must happen next.

prompt_templates.title: Check A Completion Claim
prompt_templates.prompt: Use truth-layer to verify this completion claim. Check the project state and report whether it is valid or blocked.

prompt_templates.title: Find Hidden Blockers
prompt_templates.prompt: Use truth-layer before continuing. Verify build status, tests, types, dependencies, and file state. Stop if any blocker is unresolved.

prompt_templates.title: Write A Blocker Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use truth-layer to create a blocker report. Include what failed, impact, root cause, current state, and the next action.

prompt_templates.title: Audit A Multi-Agent Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Use truth-layer to audit this handoff between agents. Compare claimed progress with evidence and identify dependent work that must stop.

output_examples.input: The build is complete and ready to deploy.
output_examples.output: Truth Layer result: blocked. The build claim needs command evidence, type-check evidence, and a deployable artifact before release can proceed.

output_examples.input: Tests pass and coverage is complete.
output_examples.output: Truth Layer result: needs verification. Count real test files, reject empty stubs, and confirm the test command produced a passing result.
Truth Layer result: verify visually. Inspect the UI state, confirm the changed workflow works, and report any blocker with the required fix.

best_practices: Run the relevant project commands before accepting completion claims.
Separate verified facts from assumptions in every report.
Stop dependent work when a blocker affects build, tests, types, or deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not mark work complete based only on file edits.
Do not count empty or placeholder tests as coverage.
Do not ignore build warnings that hide real failures.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an agent verify claims, identify blockers, and avoid premature completion reports.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: It instructs the agent to run relevant verification commands when tools and permissions allow it.

faq.question: Is this a testing framework?
faq.answer: No. It is a workflow guide for using existing builds, tests, type checks, and inspection tools.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before marking engineering work complete or before handing work to another agent or team.

faq.question: Can it verify UI changes?
faq.answer: It can guide the agent to inspect UI state with Playwright MCP when that tool is available.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it asks agents to use Bash, filesystem, git, and UI inspection tools.
